description: Address documentation gaps and inaccuracies: update Getting Started to use EKS Access Entries instead of deprecated aws-auth, add disruption candidacy documentation, create cloud provider developer guide, fix stale examples, and improve inline reference docs.

breakdown.summary: Breaking down the 79 issues into 15 distinct work categories: (1) aws-auth→EKS Access Entries migration in Getting Started (high-priority, complex, affects multiple docs); (2) Disruption candidacy documentation overhaul (needs new comprehensive guide); (3) Cloud provider developer guide (new docs, medium complexity); (4) Alpha→Beta concept cleanup across 6+ docs (mostly straightforward replacements); (5) Broken link fixes in FAQ and migration guides (trivial); (6) IAM policy and Pod Identity updates (3 docs, moderate complexity); (7) Troubleshooting process restructuring (medium effort, needs discussion on scope); (8) Well-known labels additions (trivial); (9) NodeClaim concepts doc (new, medium effort); (10) Upgrade guide completion and v1 migration fixes (several breaking changes, needs discussion); (11) Installation path clarification for existing EKS clusters (medium complexity, combines 3 related issues); (12) CRD reference/OpenAPI schema generation (new tooling, medium effort); (13) EKS-specific features (Access Entries AL2023, LoadBalancer tagging, minor fixes); (14) Spot/On-Demand capacity type clarification (small, contradictory docs); (15) EOL/support policy definition (policy decision needed before docs can be written). The 18 obvious fixes include: broken link corrections, dead anchor fixes, adding missing labels to lists, terminology corrections in specific sections, and single-line config/parameter documentation. The 61 requiring discussion span policy decisions (EOL, upgrade support guarantees), architectural guidance (scheduler mode impact, CAS coexistence), missing implementation features (Windows SNAT config, karpenter-convert List support), and high-complexity rewrites (Getting Started overhaul, troubleshooting restructuring).

description: Defer EC2NodeClass launch configuration changes requiring design decisions: multi-part MIME userdata restructuring for private registries, AWS Outposts support, dynamic EC2 tagging from pod annotations, valueFrom/fromSecret in userData, roleSelectorTerms tag-based discovery, and other features needing RFC-level discussion before implementation.

breakdown.summary: All 29 issues require substantive design decisions: (1) Multi-part MIME userdata restructuring (issues #2750, #5494, #3788, #4492, #5324) — 5 issues requiring coordinated redesign of bootstrap flow; (2) AWS feature exposure gaps (issues #7047, #8070, #2996, #8348, #3182, #6826) — 6 straightforward API field additions but need validation; (3) Dynamic tagging/templating (issues #4228, #7506, #5105, #2783, #7720) — 5 issues requiring new templating engine; (4) Resource discovery via SelectorTerms (issues #5464, #1284) — 2 issues extending tag-based discovery pattern; (5) Launch template strategy (issues #3666, #6625, #4431, #3369) — 4 issues with conflicting design goals (versioning vs. external templates); (6) Capability/lifecycle gaps (issues #3899, #6296, #3324, #1240, #5860, #3915) — 6 issues needing new hook points or architectural decisions. No trivial fixes: every issue involves either multi-part coordination, new subsystems, or policy reversal.

description: Improve test coverage: add E2E tests for multi-node consolidation/candidate filtering/metrics, add Container Insights log forwarding to test clusters, set up private cluster E2E testing via VPC-resident runner, add helm template CI validation, fix data races in E2E test infrastructure, and refactor tests to dynamically account for ambient DaemonSet overhead.

breakdown.summary: 8 distinct work streams: (1) dynamic AMI discovery, (2) webhook/metric unit tests, (3) multi-node consolidation E2E tests, (4) DaemonSet coupling refactor, (5) data race fixes, (6) private cluster E2E via VPC runner, (7) helm template CI validation, (8) scale/upgrade/compatibility test expansion. Obvious fixes: #327 (metrics unit tests), #3514 (instance provider unit tests), #4472 (remove workaround). Remaining 17 require design decisions on test architecture, infrastructure changes (Container Insights, VPC runner), and prioritization of new test scenarios.

description: Improve decision observability: fix lo.Reduce pod-count accumulator bug in disruption logs, add structured log lines for pod evictions during consolidation, emit pod-level Kubernetes Events for launch failures (ICE, account limits), add provisioning failure counter metric with reason/NodePool labels, and propagate DisruptionReason for forceful disruptions.

breakdown.summary: 8 distinct pieces of work: (1) lo.Reduce accumulator bug fix [trivial], (2) emit pod-level events for ICE/capacity errors [medium—API design], (3) structured metrics for provisioning failures with reason/NodePool labels [medium—metric design], (4) log pod evictions during consolidation [small], (5) surface DisruptionReason in forceful disruption paths [medium—state propagation], (6) emit DisruptionBlocked events in correct namespace [small], (7) improve error messages for resource limits vs. no-fit [small—UX], (8) reduce log spam for incompatible pods [medium—filtering logic]. Trivial: #2888 accumulator bug, #7176 node name in log, #2238 context to removal message. Medium/Large: #2159, #5485, #4346, #8187 (event/metric design), #2023 (forceful disruption propagation), #2016 (namespace scoping), #686, #6884, #5862, #676 (error messaging & metrics), #5487, #1904, #5004, #7083, #2020, #2332 (observability design decisions)

description: Improve CI and release automation: add make docgen to PR pipeline, add release script dry-run CI job, configure Dependabot/Renovate for pinned tool versions, fix release notes RC tag filtering, fix git tag ordering to commit chart values before tagging, and other small CI maintenance improvements.

breakdown.summary: Distinct work: (1) shell script quoting fixes, (2) dry-run CI for release scripts, (3) minor version release docs logic, (4) KWOK image pipeline, (5) ArtifactHub presence, (6) params.latest_k8s_version substitution, (7) RC tag filtering in release notes, (8) git tag/chart values ordering, (9) metrics_gen AST improvements, (10) make docgen in CI with AWS auth, (11) EKSCTL version auto-upgrade, (12) metrics subsystem variable resolution, (13) spell checking for docs, (14) release automation migrations/consolidations. Obviously correct: #5061 (shell quoting), #3488 (spell check CI), #6096 (EKSCTL version bump). Needs discussion: API changes (#5153), policy on garbage ECR tags (#7482), multi-repo release orchestration (#3225), docs folder versioning strategy (#3823), archived GitHub Action replacement (#6206), and several medium-complexity logic fixes.

description: Accept well-scoped EC2NodeClass launch configuration extensions: relax snapshotID validation for volumeInitializationRate, add NestedVirtualization to CpuOptions, add missing BlockDeviceMapping fields (virtualName, noDevice), add eks:cluster-name default tag, expose Instance Bandwidth Configuration, and extend instanceStorePolicy options for --no-bind-pods-logs. PRs are already open or in review for most of these.

breakdown.summary: Breakdown: 6 straightforward additions (NestedVirtualization, volumeName/noDevice fields, eks:cluster-name tag, InstanceMetadataTags, IBC exposure, KeyName field) are low-risk schema extensions. 8 require design decisions: validation relaxation (snapshotID), policy reconciliation (instance profile drift, role-to-profile transitions), selector enhancements (VPC scope for SGs, AZ for capacity reservations), feature toggles (blockDeviceMapping opt-out), hostname type configuration, IAM path support, DryRun context issue, and KMS/SQS integration. 2 infrastructure items (KMS SQS, IMDSv2 SCP DryRun) are deployment/permissions concerns outside core schema.

description: Fix scheduling simulation bugs: add matchLabelKeys support for topology spread constraints, fix topology.Update() registration gap during nodeAffinity relaxation causing runaway node provisioning, populate network/EBS bandwidth labels for newer instance families, model per-instance-type EBS volume attachment limits for StatefulSets, fix nil pointer panic in hashSelector, and other well-diagnosed simulation correctness issues.

breakdown.summary: All 17 issues are complex scheduling simulation bugs requiring careful design and testing. Categories: (1) Topology spread constraint bugs—matchLabelKeys support, domain aggregation scope, deprecated label normalization, minDomains inconsistency (4 issues); (2) Node affinity handling—preferred affinity strictness, restricted label rejection, first-term-only PV affinity processing (3 issues); (3) Resource accounting—EBS volume attachment limits, kubelet reserved resources, CSINode allocatable volumes (3 issues); (4) Topology state management—nil hashSelector panic, topology.Update() registration gap, ephemeral volume zone locking (3 issues); (5) Instance type label population—network/EBS bandwidth missing for newer families (1 issue); (6) Consolidation logic—pod rescheduling assumptions with StatefulSets (1 issue). Each requires investigation of simulator logic, likely touching multiple code paths, with high risk of breaking existing scheduling if implemented incorrectly. No trivial fixes.

description: Fix Helm chart and installation issues: expose containerSecurityContext/podSecurityContext as overridable values, add helm template CI validation to catch webhook.enabled=false regressions, fix v1 CRD migration failures leaving stale v1beta1 objects, update flowcontrol API version from v1beta3 to v1, add kustomization.yaml for CRDs, and address dynamic cluster name configuration (PR #7983 in progress).

breakdown.summary: Breakdown: (1) API version bump v1beta3→v1 flowcontrol + kustomization.yaml for CRDs (trivial, 2 issues); (2) RBAC conditional logic fix for webhook.enabled (1 issue, straightforward); (3) securityContext exposure in chart values (1 issue, medium complexity); (4) CRD conversion webhook configuration & dynamic cluster name (2 issues, requires design decisions); (5) v1beta1 migration & orphaned objects cleanup (2 issues, complex upgrade logic); (6) CI/testing gaps: helm-unittest + values validation (2 issues, process improvements); (7) leader-elect & garbage collection issues (2 issues, mixed complexity). Key debate points: v1beta1 storage migration strategy, conversion webhook defaults/documentation, dynamic cluster name mechanism.

description: Add early validation for cloud resources: reject ARN-formatted values in spec.role with clear error (preventing instance profile quota exhaustion), normalize ARN input to role name, detect commonly-confused label keys with warnings, add kstatus-compatible status conditions to EC2NodeClass for resource resolution state, and classify terminal vs retryable AWS errors consistently across providers.

breakdown.summary: 8 distinct pieces: (1) add kstatus-compatible READY condition to EC2NodeClass, (2) reject/normalize ARN in spec.role field, (3) reject/normalize ARN in instanceProfile field, (4) validate security groups resolve to actual resources, (5) classify AWS errors as terminal vs retryable, (6) warn on commonly-confused label keys (eks.amazonaws.com/capacityType), (7) fix InstanceProfileReady condition logic for missing role and pre-existing profiles, (8) AWS auth credential health checks. Obviously correct: security group existence validation at admission (#2932), label key mismatch warnings (#5521). Needs discussion: caching TTL strategy for IAM propagation (#8114), error misclassification scope (#8148), instance profile quota exhaustion root cause (#8838), pod readiness probe behavior (#3351).

description: Defer scheduling simulation features requiring design work: capacity reservation integration, alignment between consolidation simulation and kube-scheduler scoring, percentage-based capacity type distribution, fractional/partitioned GPU semantics (RFC needed), cluster-level default TopologySpreadConstraints, and provisioner-consolidation coordination. All need RFCs.

breakdown.summary: All 11 issues require design decisions and RFCs: 3 architectural coordination problems (provisioner-consolidation sync, consolidation-scheduler alignment, reserved-capacity reassignment logic), 4 scheduling simulation gaps (default topology spread, GPU fractional semantics, volume limits, offering-dependent requirements), 2 resource modeling issues (GPU Feature Discovery mutations, request-limit averaging), 1 capacity distribution policy (spot/on-demand percentages), 1 reserved-capacity over-reservation trade-off. None are trivial fixes; each involves either new API concepts, simulation algorithm changes, or fundamental coordination between independent subsystems.

description: Defer spot lifecycle improvements requiring design work: configurable spot-to-spot consolidation thresholds, delay spot-interruption termination until replacement Ready, AZ-aware consolidation, Rebalance Recommendation Notification (RBN) support, and feature flag graduation. Each requires an RFC to evaluate tradeoffs between cost savings, interruption risk, and operational safety.

Fix Bottlerocket settings handling: change TOML merge order so user-specified fields take precedence over auto-discovered values, wire imageGC thresholds to correct filesystem device for separate data volumes, implement map[string]interface{} passthrough to avoid silently dropping valid settings not in hardcoded schema, and fix integer/string type handling for GC threshold fields.

Defer NodeOverlay design-dependent features: per-instance-type VM memory overhead (awaiting NodeOverlay for correct override mechanism), custom/extended resource scheduling (awaiting NodeOverlay or DRA), observability RFC for overlay status conditions/metrics, and stateful capacity-tracking for Savings Plans pricing. All need design work.

Defer pod-level disruption control enhancements requiring RFCs: graceful consolidation mode for do-not-disrupt pods, consolidateAfter filter for CronJob/ephemeral pods, namespace-level disruption blocking, WorkloadDisruptionBudget integration (KEP-4671), and surge eviction semantics (KEP-4565). Multiple competing designs exist with no consensus.

Defer global/cross-NodePool resource limits: cluster-wide limit CRD, reservation/Savings Plan capacity integration, time-based/scheduled limits, and cost-based limits. All require new API objects and design docs. Maintainers have explicitly requested RFCs.

Add missing kubelet configuration fields: imageMaximumGCAge passthrough, kubelet verbosity/logLevel in EC2NodeClass, fix kubeReserved calculation to respect user-defined MaxPods instead of unconditionally using ENI-based limits, and account for reservedSystemCPUs in scheduling simulation.

Defer disruption budget features requiring design work: budget-respecting soft expiration (multiple competing designs), reason-scoped budget consumption semantics, maintenance window semantics, minAge/disruptAfter field, configurable auto-repair suppression thresholds, and distinguishing involuntary vs voluntary budget consumption. RFCs needed for all.

Defer instance type label features requiring design: additional NodePool metric labels (cardinality concerns unresolved), re-introduction of max-pods label (deliberately removed, needs RFC), processor feature labels (API stability concerns), and BaselinePerformanceFactors label.

Defer kubelet configuration expansions requiring RFC-level design: IMDS-derived runtime value injection in AL2023 NodeConfig, configurable maxPods offset for non-kube-proxy CNI stacks, expression-based kubelet fields (maxPodsExpression, systemReserved) resolving at launch time, and maxPods min-vs-override semantics.

Defer stuck node features requiring design work: configurable registration TTL (RFC PR #1989 open), NodeRepair feature graduation (blocked on #2042 and per-NodePool repair controls #2811), and upstream kernel regression detection heuristics.

Fix well-scoped observability issues: correct Grafana dashboard panel titles and calculation bugs, add job label filter to dashboard queries, add managed/unmanaged label to interruption message metric, and expose AWS SDK ClientLogMode via debug flag. PRs already open or in progress for most items.

Defer unthemed issues requiring design: push/event-driven CloudProvider interface refactor, per-NodePool batching configuration, self-hosting/bootstrap CLI, and new instance family tracking (awaiting GA availability and codegen run).

Defer pricing model features requiring design work: cost/dollar-based NodePool limits, Savings Plan/Reserved Instance awareness, alternative pricing data sources for private VPCs, and multi-cluster pricing coordination. All depend on NodeOverlay or require RFCs for API integration and cross-cutting design decisions.

Fix disruption budget enforcement bugs: count in-flight disruptions in BuildDisruptionBudgetMapping to prevent budget violations from racing reconcile cycles, fix disrupted-node counter to include nodes with deletion timestamps, correct reason-scoped budget evaluation and event messaging, and add PDB-aware candidate selection to prevent permanent Unconsolidatable marking when multiple candidates share a PDB.

Defer proactive/static capacity features: EBS warm pool management, static NodePool status conditions for readiness, scale-down node selection controls, warm pool pre-initialization, and CapacityBuffer/headroom APIs. All require new subsystems or API surface with no RFC in place. CapacityBuffer has active RFC discussion in #2571.

Defer multi-node consolidation enhancements requiring design work: consolidationGroup API or algorithmic improvements, m→n consolidation (replacing N nodes with M>1), and improved cross-node candidate heuristics. Multiple POC PRs exist exploring algorithm-level fixes; an RFC is needed before committing to API surface or algorithmic changes.

Defer disruption cost evaluation features requiring RFCs: node-age as disruption candidate sorting criterion, configurable consolidation savings thresholds (RFC PR #2562 in progress), price-performance ranking, and utilization threshold/minimum-savings checks. Users can work around via drift, disruption budgets, NodeOverlay, or pod eviction cost annotations.

Fix stale-state race conditions: re-validate pod emptiness before NodeClaim deletion to prevent TOCTOU races with actively-binding pods, re-fetch NodePool and re-check readiness before creating NodeClaim, eagerly call cluster.UpdateNode() after patching NoSchedule taint to close the informer sync window, and fix duplicate 'marking consolidatable' log lines from read-after-write inconsistency.

Fix DaemonSet pod handling: skip DaemonSet-owned pods when evaluating drain completion in termination controller, compute per-instance-type DaemonSet overhead instead of using provider-level aggregation, inject guaranteed Karpenter lifecycle labels into simulated node during DaemonSet overhead calculation, and exclude DaemonSet pods from disruption cost calculations.

Fix stuck/zombie node issues with clear paths: treat stopped/stopping EC2 instances as non-viable in GC controller, formalize force-terminate annotation as public API (PR in progress), add EC2 instance health polling via DescribeInstanceStatus for Simplified Automatic Recovery detection, improve error messaging for NodeClass deletion deadlocks, and document AL2023 EC2_LINUX_BOOTSTRAPPING access entry requirement.

Fix drift detection issues: change drift evaluation to semantic requirement check instead of hash comparison to avoid unnecessary mass disruptions, fix security group drift to use subset check instead of exact match, fix NodeRegistrationHealthy condition reset on irrelevant field changes, fix label merge order in launch.go so provider-resolved labels take precedence, and extend drift cleanup to unlaunched/failing NodeClaims. PR already open for Drifted status condition.

Fix ephemeral storage calculation bugs: correct Bottlerocket rootVolume capacity computation for two-volume designs, honor EC2NodeClass blockDeviceMappings volume size overrides in scheduling simulation, merge user-specified mappings with AMI-family defaults instead of all-or-nothing replacement, and resolve snapshot sizes via DescribeSnapshots when volumeSize is unspecified.

Fix well-scoped spot lifecycle bugs: treat InvalidFleetConfiguration as capacity unavailability signal, fall back to on-demand pricing for local zones missing spot price history, fix DisruptionReason omission in Interruption Controller, tighten toleration matching to prevent skipping graceful eviction for wildcard-tolerating pods, and differentiate on-demand scheduled maintenance events from terminal spot reclamations to respect do-not-disrupt annotations.

Defer zone-health-aware provisioning features: subnet IP availability filtering, zone-level health modeling for provisioning, ARC Zonal Shift integration, and zone-balancing behavior. All require RFCs to resolve design tradeoffs. Community sample controllers provide interim workarounds.

Gather diagnostic info for stuck node issues: confirm whether NodeRepair feature gate resolves Unknown/NotReady disruption states, verify reproduction of PDB-blocked health-triggered terminations on recent versions, check if NMA fix resolves LastTransitionTime oscillation, and confirm whether newer Karpenter versions resolve registration-TTL termination skipping cordon+drain.

Defer dynamic volume sizing features requiring design work: dynamically sizing block device mappings based on pod ephemeral-storage requests, conditional storage configuration based on instance store availability, and image pre-caching workflows dependent on upstream split-image-filesystem KEP. No RFC exists for the API shape or algorithm.

Fix pricing model bugs: eliminate negative karpenter_nodepools_cost_total by recomputing as full sum instead of delta bookkeeping, flush instance type cache on seqnum change to prevent heap bloat from dead entries, and add pricing API region override env var with graceful fallback for SCP-restricted environments.

Defer Windows features blocked on upstream platform: IPv6 support (awaiting AWS EKS platform support), and Custom AMIFamily Windows detection (design doc needed for DescribeImages-based platform detection touching scheduling loop).

Defer terminate-before-create and replace-then-evict disruption strategies. Both invert fundamental safety assumptions and require careful design around PDB enforcement, budget accounting, and launch failure modes. Maintainers have redirected some aspects to sig-apps/PDB. No RFC exists for either approach.

Defer AMI selection features requiring design alignment: per-architecture/accelerator AMI pinning within a single EC2NodeClass, version-aware custom AMI selection, alias-equivalent requirement injection for direct selectors, and AMI family as a node label. Multiple competing design proposals need RFC resolution.

Defer observability enhancements requiring upstream alignment or broader design: drift state in NodePool.Status (belongs in upstream kubernetes-sigs/karpenter), launch/registration failure reason metric labels (awaiting-more-evidence), spot interruption NodeClaim condition API shape, and KEP-5619 autoscaler pod condition adoption (blocked on upstream KEP).

Fix AMI selection issues: derive architecture requirements from resolved AMI set to filter incompatible instance types (draft PR in progress), add debug logging for SSM query paths and failures (PR #7940 in review), and add TTL cache for SSM GetParameter calls to prevent throttling.

Add well-scoped instance type labels: network-optimized boolean capability label, local storage type labels (instance-local-ssd, instance-local-hdd) from DescribeInstanceTypes DiskInfo.Type, and auto-inject custom label keys from KWOK instance-types.json.

Defer in-place update features: ReplaceRootVolume drift remediation, field-level in-place drift classification, and direct mutable field propagation to existing NodeClaims. All are behavioral changes to core drift subsystem requiring RFCs to define safety semantics.

Defer DaemonSet handling features requiring design work: provisioning trigger for pending DaemonSet pods on full nodes, handling admission-mutated DaemonSet resource requests, per-NodeClaim DaemonSet tracking for podAffinity resolution, and duck-typing daemon-like workloads. All need RFCs.

Defer cross-cutting observability instrumentation requiring design decisions: OTEL tracing for scheduling/consolidation, FleetID logging level, and high-cardinality metric opt-in/tier separation. No RFC exists for sampling strategy, span granularity, or default scrape profile changes.

Defer release automation overhauls: mega-issue covering tagging ordering, patch release automation, and doc generation fixes needs sub-issue decomposition. Also defer node-latency-for-k8s integration and local GHA testing tooling.

Defer large-cluster scaling improvements requiring design work: dynamic consolidation timeouts, configurable NodeClaim reconciler concurrency/rate limiting, and provisioning loop batching/back-pressure. All are marked awaiting-more-evidence with no RFC. Workarounds exist (increase resource limits, tune existing global settings).

Defer multi-cluster, multi-region, and cross-account features. All require fundamental architectural changes (per-region clients, namespaced CRDs, multi-kubeconfig support, per-NodeClass IAM role assumption). No RFCs exist.

Defer wave-based drift replacement batching and bake periods. Prior implementation attempt closed due to race condition; the behavioral change requires RFC to define taint scope, wave semantics, and interaction with consolidation. Workaround exists via disruption budgets.

Fix node termination lifecycle bugs: resolve EC2NodeClass deletion deadlock where finalizer waits for NodeClaims that can't terminate (separate involuntary termination path from voluntary disruption checks), add ENI deletion retry with status check for orphaned ENIs post-termination, and downgrade eviction-queue ERROR logs for already-deleted NodeClaims to DEBUG.

Fix NodeOverlay operational issues: add gauge metric for invalid/not-ready NodeOverlays, fix single misconfigured NodePool blocking all provisioning cluster-wide (continue loop on per-NodePool errors), downgrade transient startup initialization ERROR logs to debug level, and document price field units and multi-overlay composition rules.

Count all non-terminal NodeClaims (pending, launched, unregistered) against NodePool resource limits during provisioning decisions. Currently, limits are silently bypassed when nodes fail to register or during rapid scale-up races, enabling unbounded cost explosion. Fix spans scheduler accounting, ExceededBy off-by-one, and concurrent creation races. A working PR already exists.

Fix Windows node support bugs: correct eviction hard threshold from 100Mi to 500Mi for Windows (PR #8852 open), fix MaxPods calculation for multi-ENI/prefix delegation on Windows, and extend instance type suppression for known Windows-incompatible families (dl1, f1, inf2, etc.).

Validate stale-state issues: confirm whether the 15-second revalidation window is genuinely insufficient for pod binding races, verify if consolidation regression from karpenter-core#458 is still present, and check if PVC watch handler fix (#1294) already resolved provisioner re-enqueue for PVC transitions.

Defer large-scale testing infrastructure: KwoK-based scale test harnesses, performance metric emission into PR checks, and E2E failure aggregation dashboards. All require design decisions and ongoing investment.

Defer low-priority pod exclusion from provisioning/consolidation (analogous to cluster-autoscaler expendable-pods-priority-cutoff), surge-awareness/headroom mechanisms, and CapacityBuffer/virtual pod integration. Maintainers have requested an RFC due to non-trivial interactions with consolidation loops.

Gather diagnostic info for DaemonSet simulation bugs: confirm root cause of incorrect node sizing with permanent taints + startupTaints, verify DaemonSet classification for WhenEmpty policy after v1.3.3 upgrade, and confirm isDaemonPodCompatible AllowUndefinedWellKnownLabels behavior.

Investigate termination lifecycle issues needing diagnosis: EKS 1.34 kubelet graceful shutdown race with DaemonSet scheduling (confirm whether Karpenter or upstream scheduler is responsible), terminationGracePeriod not triggering on stuck nodes (confirm NodeClaim-level configuration), and disruption taint blocking by intolerant third-party pods (confirm whether drain-blocking events are already emitted).

Apply small repository improvements: declarative Probot Settings App configuration for repo settings, wrap error returns with resource name context for debuggability, and add node-role.kubernetes.io to permitted label prefix allowlist.

Defer cluster-wide disruption controls: label-based disruption budget grouping, cluster-wide circuit breaker CRD, and dynamic CEL-based budget validation for multi-provider support. Multiple competing designs exist with no consensus; RFCs are needed.

Defer Helm chart improvements requiring design decisions: ArgoCD/GitOps-compatible v1 migration ordering, default ServiceMonitor metric drop rules vs documentation approach, and multi-cluster chart packaging patterns.

Defer external node adoption and ASG lifecycle management features. Both require new CRDs, reconciliation loops, and resolution of deep assumptions about NodeClaim lifecycle. No RFC or design doc exists. The v1beta1 linking mechanism was removed with no replacement.

Defer graceful uninstall and cascade delete features. Both require design decisions around Helm pre-delete hooks vs controller shutdown paths vs hook Jobs, and automatic grace-period propagation semantics. RFCs needed before implementation.

Fix AWS-partition-specific pricing issues requiring AWS team access: extend codegen pipeline to support aws-cn partition for accurate China region pricing data, and merge PR #8172 for pricing API region override.

Gather diagnostic info for ambiguous spot issues: confirm isolatedVPC flag behavior for GovCloud spot pricing calls, and collect consolidation events from OD nodes to distinguish misconfiguration from real provisioning fallback bugs.

Fix startup taint lifecycle issues: add timeout for startup taint removal to detect daemon failures and trigger replacement, and filter readiness.k8s.io-prefixed taints from Karpenter's startup taint tracking to avoid conflicts with K8s 1.36 Node Readiness Controller.

Defer startup taint features requiring design: new onlyEnforceIfPresent API field for cluster-scoped readiness gating config, re-application of startupTaints after node restarts, and configurable registration TTL. All need RFCs to define semantics and resolve taint ownership lifecycle.

Defer documentation infrastructure work requiring cross-team agreement: versioned doc hosting architecture, archived doc site for older releases, and multi-provider doc organization. Active negotiation is underway but no concrete proposal has landed.

Fix MaxPods formula to account for VPC CNI Prefix Delegation on Linux nodes (PR #7082 exists), and fix trunking-compatibility list sync with EKS platform version to prevent pods stuck Pending due to missing pod-eni capacity.

Fix race condition where do-not-disrupt pods get evicted due to taint application timing. Apply NoSchedule taint before validation period, wait for informer cache sync, then re-validate. Also differentiate future-dated retirement events from imminent spot interruptions to avoid premature eviction. Partial mitigation landed in v0.36.1; complete the fix for remaining edge cases.

Verify whether metric registration issue is already fixed on current main branch (contributor noted it may be resolved), and request meaningful description for empty KWOK DRA driver test issue before it can be triaged.

Investigate Helm install issues needing diagnosis: empty karpenter-cert secret after ArgoCD-managed upgrade (webhook TLS broken), and RBAC aggregation ClusterRole toggle awaiting a concrete PR proposal.

Investigate disruption budget issues needing diagnosis: confirm whether 10% budget rounding to 0 or DaemonSet classification regression is the root cause of 60+ empty nodes not being deleted after v1.3.3 upgrade. Maintainer has requested debug logs. Also confirm need for disruption budget status in NodePool (accepted but needs lightweight API design).

Investigate provisioning failures needing diagnosis: confirm whether EKS Auto Mode bugs are caused by missing AmazonEKSComputePolicy IAM attachment, and verify whether v1.9.0 CRD schema enum mismatch is a real code bug vs upgrade sequencing issue.

Defer selective drift exclusion for userData fields (e.g., rotating bearer tokens) and externally-mutated label suppression. Multiple design options exist (variable templating, field-level exclusion, reference-based hashing, trusted label prefixes) with no consensus. An RFC is needed to evaluate tradeoffs. Workaround available via fork carry commit.

Defer gang-scheduling support. Atomic pod-group provisioning, queue-blocked pod filtering, and cross-pod PVC zone constraint awareness are cross-cutting changes to core provisioning logic requiring a design doc. Basic interoperability with Volcano/YuniKorn works today.

Defer NLB deregistration delay handling and termination sequence changes during node termination. Design uncertainty exists around the right primitive (minNodeDrainTime, NLB API integration, finalizer-based signal). Workaround exists via DaemonSet terminationGracePeriodSeconds.
Defer Server Side Apply adoption across all controllers. Cross-cutting change requiring careful field manager design per object type. Deprioritized to awaiting-more-evidence with no RFC.
Defer NodePool suspension/deprecation feature. Behavioral change to provisioning and drift subsystems with no RFC. Workarounds exist (drift budget 0, taint existing nodes, external workload scaling).

Fix permanently unschedulable pods (no matching NodePool) acting as a permanent veto on all cluster-wide consolidation and disruption, causing runaway node provisioning and cost explosion. Distinguish between pods unschedulable due to proposed disruption vs already provably unschedulable regardless — only the former should block disruption.

Defer DRA (Dynamic Resource Allocation) integration until upstream APIs reach beta stability (targeting K8s 1.32+). The work is cross-cutting (CloudProvider InstanceType model, ResourceClaim awareness, CEL-based simulation) and requires an RFC.

Defer custom disruption trigger mechanism for user-defined node replacement signals (network throttling, compliance). Requires either upstream DRA network resource support or new annotation-based disruption Method — both need RFCs. Workaround exists via kubectl delete node.

Defer Windows prefix delegation support. Requires mirroring existing Linux logic for MaxPods calculation, bootstrap argument generation, and VPC CNI config detection — enough moving parts to warrant a design note.

Fix AWS-specific kubelet configuration issues requiring AWS team access: NODEADM_KUBELET_ARGS quoting bug in AL2023 userdata generation that swallows user-supplied flags. An AWS engineer is already tracking the upstream fix.

Fix consolidation throughput for 3000+ node clusters where sequential processing and budget-blocked empty-node deletion cause unacceptable latency. Requires parallelizing empty-node deletion path and adding observability for budget-blocked nodes.

Defer InPlacePodVerticalScaling integration. Requires coordinated changes to capacity model, consolidation, and provisioning simulation. Upstream API only reached beta in K8s 1.33. RFC needed to address consolidation thrash loops and stale capacity issues.
Confirm whether the existing Kubernetes event emitted when drift is blocked by scheduling constraints is sufficient, or if an additional structured log entry at debug level is still desired.

Defer informer handler performance optimization for high-churn clusters. Root cause is synchronous handler execution on FIFO pop path; fix requires handler consolidation or async offloading with design consideration.

Fix consolidation candidate ranking where binary nomination exclusion causes a heavily-loaded node to be deleted instead of a nearly-empty one due to cronjob nominations. Change nomination from hard block to weighted cost penalty in candidate selection.

Investigate reported 8-GPU scheduling failure: verify whether p3/g4 8-GPU instance types appear in Karpenter's instance type inventory with correct GPU counts, or if the issue is metadata/filtering rather than scheduling logic.

Clarify NodeRepair custom repair policy request: determine whether custom health conditions beyond built-in Ready are still desired as a feature, given that the NodeClaim Unknown status bug is tracked separately in #7494.

Fix the algorithmic bug in multi-node consolidation where the hard 100-node cap and binary search strategy systematically starve candidates in the lower portion of the sorted array. A maintainer has triaged as important-soon with a related performance PR in flight.

Investigate reported case of ~90% utilized node being disrupted and replaced with identical instance type. Maintainer suspects drift or expiration rather than consolidation; logs/events needed to confirm root cause before prescribing a fix.
